revision_metadata.summary: Iteration 2 fixes 7 QA findings + 9 codex iter-2-review findings. QA fixes: (OBJ-01) AC-02 adds 4-field echo contract to prove '读+行动'; (OBJ-02) AC-04(g) split into g.script + g.hook; (OBJ-03) new AC-05 for spec-continue chain; (OBJ-04) Path B removed; (OBJ-05) auth flag consumed at observation; (OBJ-06) stale-grep extended to dev.md:898 + close.md:416; (OBJ-07) full 20-entry negative-delta enumeration. Codex iter-2-post-codex fixes: (#1) AC-02 requires independent sha256 recomputation of digest + score_injection_action min 20 chars + reject placeholder values like 'no action needed'; (#2) AC-04(g.script) tightened to main-agent-only execution with QA validating captured evidence (no agent_id-stripping wrappers); (#3) AC-05 Phase B requires REAL end-to-end commit (stub option removed, marked supplemental only); (#4) AC-05 ac_deviation_allowed downgraded to false; deferral requires external user-consent artifact citation; (#5) context.json requirements_decomposition R7 updated to map to AC-05 + validation_approach now includes AC-05; (#6) M1/AC-01 reworded: cycle-scenario sum is PRIMARY check; per-event sweep is GUARD only (per-event cap alone does NOT guarantee cycle-total cap); (#7) M4.3 hook deny extended to ALL model tool calls regardless of agent_id (main agent NO LONGER exempt); auth flag legitimately created only by human terminal; AC-04 g.script.2/.3 setup uses CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE env var; g_hook_5 case flipped from expected-zero to expected-nonzero; (#8) try/finally wording fixed to unlink-immediately-at-observation-BEFORE-write (not in finally block); (#9) negative-delta preservation wording scoped to negative-VALUED entries only, not all entries in events containing any negative.

criteria.title: Cycle-total positive-score cap +5 across {dev,ba,qa} via Path A (M1)
criteria.given: scripts/score-update.sh is read AND stale delta comments in commands/dev.md:897-898 and commands/close.md:415-419 AND any tests/generated/<prior-task-id>/test_AC_01_score_rebalance_01.py files are reviewed
criteria.when: positive EVENT_DELTAS entries are summed across {dev,ba,qa} for the maximum cycle scenario AND every positive event's cycle-sum is computed AND every negative entry is compared to the pre-change snapshot
criteria.then: (a) the EVENT_DELTAS dict in scripts/score-update.sh is rebalanced (Path A: static rebalance — Path B accumulator REMOVED in iteration 2 per OBJ-04) so that for EVERY positive event in {qa_first_pass, qa_second_pass, close_success_qa_pass, close_success_qa_fail_fixed, user_rating_5}, the SUM across {dev,ba,qa} is <= +5 AND the cycle-scenario sums (defined in `check.scenarios_to_verify_primary`) are <= +5 (codex finding #6: cycle-scenario sum is the PRIMARY check; per-event sum is a GUARD); (b) ALL NEGATIVE-VALUED ENTRIES in EVENT_DELTAS (i.e., every (event, agent) pair where the pre-change value is < 0) remain unchanged after the iter-2 dev edits — the full enumeration is the 20 entries in `check.negative_deltas_unchanged_full_enumeration`. Codex finding #9: this wording explicitly scopes preservation to NEGATIVE-VALUED entries only — zero-valued companions in events that also contain negative values (e.g., qa_reject_dev qa=0) are NOT in the preservation set and may legitimately remain or be changed; (c) commands/dev.md:897 (qa_first_pass) AND commands/dev.md:898 (qa_second_pass) AND commands/dev.md:1151-1152 documentation comments reflect the new Path A values (zero matches for stale strings listed in `check.stale_strings_to_grep_out`); (d) commands/close.md:415 (close_success_qa_pass) AND commands/close.md:416 (close_success_qa_fail_fixed) documentation comments reflect the new Path A values; (e) any stale tests/generated/<prior-task-id>/test_AC_01_score_rebalance_01.py either has its expected values updated to the new table or is documented as superseded in the dev-report; (f) docs/dev/dev-report-20260524-205206.json declares `score_rebalance_path_chosen: "A"` (Path B removed from M1 this iteration)

check._path_mandate_note: OBJ-04 fix: Path B removed from M1 in iteration 2. AC-01 verifies Path A static-read only — EVENT_DELTAS dict values are summed directly. Eliminates Path A/B drift between spec and AC verification.

scenarios_to_verify_primary._status: PRIMARY — this is the authoritative cycle-total check
scenarios_to_verify_primary.rule: For each scenario, compute (sum over events e in scenario, sum over agents a in {dev,ba,qa}, EVENT_DELTAS[e][a]) and assert <= 5.

per_event_positive_sum_sweep_guard._status: GUARD — additional safety check, NOT a substitute for the cycle-scenario check above
per_event_positive_sum_sweep_guard.rule: For EVERY event E in EVENT_DELTAS where ANY agent has a positive value, the sum (dev_value + ba_value + qa_value) MUST be <= 5
per_event_positive_sum_sweep_guard.rationale_corrected: Per-event positive cap is a GUARD against stray non-zero values, NOT a guarantee of cycle-total cap (codex finding #6: capping each of N events at +5 individually only bounds cycle-total at N*5, not 5). The cycle-scenario check above is the authoritative gate.

check._negative_deltas_full_note: OBJ-07 fix: prior iteration enumerated 7 entries; this is the full 20-entry snapshot (all entries with value < 0 across the EVENT_DELTAS dict at git HEAD pre-iter-2-dev-edits). For every event in this list, the post-change value MUST equal the listed value exactly. Source-of-truth snapshot is scripts/score-update.sh EVENT_DELTAS at git HEAD prior to dev's M1 edits (commit-pinned via pre-change Read captured in dev-report).

stale_strings_to_grep_out._note: qa_first_pass parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value (dev:0, ba:0, qa:0); old `(dev +6, ba +3, qa 0.)` text must be replaced
OBJ-06 addition: qa_second_pass parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value (dev:0); old `(dev +3.)` text must be replaced
close_success_qa_pass parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value; old `(dev +5, ba +3, qa +3.)` text must be replaced
OBJ-06 addition: close_success_qa_fail_fixed parenthetical comment must reflect new Path A value; old `(dev +5, ba +3, qa +2.)` text must be replaced

criteria.evidence_required: static read of EVENT_DELTAS dict; per-event positive-sum sweep (all 5 positive events); full enumeration check of all 20 negative entries against the pre-change snapshot captured in the dev-report; grep for the 4 stale-string sites (dev.md:897, dev.md:898, close.md:415, close.md:416)

criteria.title: Verifiable score-injection consumption contract — read AND act (M2)
criteria.given: scripts/score-inject.sh is run against three fixture scores files (populated, missing, malformed) AND agents/ba.md, agents/dev.md, agents/qa.md are read AND docs/dev/qa-report-20260524-205206.json is produced this cycle
criteria.when: the injection text from each fixture run and the agent artifact fields are inspected
criteria.then: (a) every stdout branch of score-inject.sh (normal at lines ~104-106, missing-file fallback at lines ~38-43, malformed-file fallback at lines ~67-75) contains the literal token `INJECTION_PROOF:` followed by an instruction citing FOUR JSON fields: `rank_acknowledged`, `range_acknowledged`, `recent_events_digest_acknowledged`, AND `score_injection_action`; (b) the INJECTION_PROOF line substitutes the actual <RANK_LABEL>, <RANGE>, and <RECENT_EVENTS_DIGEST> (8-hex-char prefix of sha256 over the recent-events string emitted by score-inject) for the run (no unsubstituted angle-brackets remain); (c) agents/ba.md, agents/dev.md, agents/qa.md each contain a section titled `Score-injection echo contract` (or token-equivalent) instructing the agent to include FOUR JSON fields in its primary output artifact (BA: context JSON; Dev: dev-report JSON; QA: qa-report JSON): `"rank_acknowledged": <string>`, `"range_acknowledged": <string>`, `"recent_events_digest_acknowledged": <8-char hex string — sha256 prefix of the recent-events string the agent received>`, AND `"score_injection_action": <non-empty free-text string, 1 line, length >= 20 chars, MUST cite at least one concrete injection signal (rank label / range bucket / named recent-event) AND at least one concrete behavioral adjustment, MUST NOT be a placeholder value like 'no action needed' / 'n/a' / 'none' / 'ok' (full list in check.score_injection_action_spec.forbidden_placeholder_values_case_insensitive)>`; (d) the contract section in each agent file includes the fail-safe note: if injection absent/malformed, set ALL FOUR fields to null; do not fabricate; (e) tests/score-inject-contract/test-inject-branches.sh exists, is executable, and exits 0 when run against the three fixtures — asserting INJECTION_PROOF token, all four field names, AND INDEPENDENTLY recomputing sha256[:8] over the actual emitted recent-events text and asserting equality with the substituted <RECENT_EVENTS_DIGEST> (NOT merely a valid-hex regex check — codex finding #1 fix); (f) tests/score-inject-contract/runtime-verify.sh exists, is executable, and when run with `--qa-report docs/dev/qa-report-20260524-205206.json --expected-rank <captured> --expected-range <captured> --captured-inject-text <path>` (i) jq-extracts all FOUR fields; (ii) asserts rank_acknowledged and range_acknowledged match captured values exactly; (iii) INDEPENDENTLY recomputes sha256[:8] over the captured recent-events text and asserts equality with .recent_events_digest_acknowledged (codex finding #1 fix); (iv) asserts score_injection_action is non-null, length >= 20, lowercase-stripped form does NOT match any forbidden placeholder value; (v) exits 0 iff all above hold

check._objection_rationale: User requirement R2 verbatim: '验证 agent 在被注入后真的会读 + 行动' (verify agent reads AND acts after injection). Iter 1 contract only required rank_acknowledged + range_acknowledged — both low-entropy (5 buckets each) and mechanically copy-able from the injected header, proving at most 'read' (or just 'echo'). Iter 2 adds (i) recent_events_digest_acknowledged (sha256 8-char prefix over DYNAMIC content — forces the agent to process the payload, not just echo labels) and (ii) score_injection_action (non-empty free-text — forces the agent to articulate a behavioral consequence, proving '行动' / act).
check.agent_contract_failsafe_required: set ALL FOUR fields to null when injection absent/malformed; do not fabricate

recent_events_digest_spec.input: the literal text of the recent-events section emitted by score-inject for this run (the bulleted history block — whatever score-inject prints as 'Recent events:' content)
recent_events_digest_spec.output_format: first 8 hex chars of the lowercase hex digest
recent_events_digest_spec.rationale: High-entropy 32-bit fingerprint computed over DYNAMIC content (recent events differ run-to-run) — proves the agent processed the injection payload.

score_injection_action_spec.constraint: free-text 1-line description that MUST (i) cite at least ONE concrete signal from the injection (a specific rank label, range bucket, or named recent-event verbatim or paraphrased), AND (ii) cite at least ONE concrete behavioral adjustment the agent took or will take this run
score_injection_action_spec.rationale: Free-text floor proves 'act' (行动). Iter-2-post-codex (codex #1 finding): raw 10-char min was gameable via 'no action needed'. Iter-2-post-codex tightens: (a) length floor 20 chars; (b) must reference a concrete injection signal AND a concrete behavior adjustment; (c) reject placeholder values verbatim. The runtime verifier in tests/score-inject-contract/runtime-verify.sh MUST: (1) extract score_injection_action; (2) lowercase + strip whitespace; (3) reject if it matches (== or is wholly contained by trimming punctuation) any forbidden_placeholder_values; (4) emit a 'low quality action description' warning if it does not contain any of the actual rank label / range string / a recent-event keyword from the injected text. The warning is informational; the hard fail is the placeholder rejection + length floor.

recent_events_digest_recomputation_requi.rule: The test fixture runner (test-inject-branches.sh) AND the runtime verifier (runtime-verify.sh) MUST INDEPENDENTLY parse the actual recent-events text emitted by score-inject.sh in each branch, recompute sha256(text).hexdigest()[:8], and assert the substituted <RECENT_EVENTS_DIGEST> in INJECTION_PROOF EQUALS the recomputed value. This prevents the script-side bug where score-inject substitutes a random or stale 8-hex string that happens to be valid hex but is not actually derived from the recent-events text.
recent_events_digest_recomputation_requi.test_implementation_sketch: In test-inject-branches.sh: capture full stdout of score-inject.sh; extract the 'Recent events:' block (or whatever label the script emits) via a known delimiter or section header; pipe through `sha256sum | cut -c1-8`; assert equals the digest cited on the INJECTION_PROOF line.
recent_events_digest_recomputation_requi.runtime_verifier_implementation_sketch: In runtime-verify.sh: read the captured score-inject output snapshot (saved at dispatch time per QA contract), extract recent-events text, recompute digest, assert equals .recent_events_digest_acknowledged jq-extracted from the qa-report.

runtime_artifact_to_jq_verify.match_against: captured score-inject output from QA dispatch this cycle; rank/range/digest must match exactly; score_injection_action must be non-null and length >= 10

criteria.ac_deviation_reason_pattern: Dev may scope this cycle's runtime verification (test (f)) to QA only because BA and Dev for this cycle are dispatched BEFORE the score-inject patch lands; their report JSONs cannot prove future-cycle compliance. Static fixture test (e) covers the script side regardless of dispatch order. This split was authorized by codex review (cycle iteration 1) and re-confirmed in iteration 2.
criteria.evidence_required: test-inject-branches.sh stdout capture + runtime-verify.sh stdout capture + jq output of QA report (all 4 fields) + grep confirmation of all 4 field names + section title in each of agents/ba.md, agents/dev.md, agents/qa.md

criteria.title: /close Session Summary as chronological timeline (M3)
criteria.given: commands/close.md is read in the region around lines 421-431 (the Session self-summary block)
criteria.when: the Session Summary template is inspected
criteria.then: (a) the heading is `## Session Summary` (or the in-text label `Session Summary` if the close template uses prose rather than markdown headings); (b) the template MANDATES a chronological timeline format where each entry has two lines: `User need: <text>` and `Design response: <text>`; (c) the template names `docs/dev/user-requirement-<DEV_SESSION_ID>.md` (and conversation-level user clarifications) as the primary source for `User need` lines, AND names dev-report / qa-report / close-report as the source for `Design response` lines only; (d) the template specifies total length <= 15 lines AND explicitly forbids narrative paragraphs; (e) the template notes that content quality is orchestrator-improvisational — the spec constrains FORMAT only; (f) the prior 5-bucket free-form text (accomplished / not accomplished / satisfied / not satisfied / bugs / improvements) is REMOVED or supplanted such that the orchestrator cannot legitimately produce the old format

check._forbidden_phrases_note: These six phrases (verbatim from current close.md:425-429) must NOT appear as the template's required content directives. They may still appear elsewhere in close.md as historical context if the dev keeps the old text as a removed-block reference.

criteria.evidence_required: static read of commands/close.md Session Summary block + grep for required phrases + grep for absence of old-format phrases in the template directives

criteria.title: User-gated /commit --bulk via auth flag + hook path guards (M4)
criteria.given: scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py is read AND commands/commit.md Step 1 (and Step 5 BULK branch) is read AND hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh (or pretool-write-guard.sh) is read AND hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh is read AND TWO separate runtime tests are run (see (g.script) and (g.hook) below)
criteria.when: each layer's behavior is inspected and BOTH runtime tests are executed
criteria.then: (a) scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py main() checks /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-${sid}.flag BEFORE writing the sentinel; if absent, prints to stderr the literal message `BULK mode requires explicit user authorization. The user (not an agent) must run: touch /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID.flag` and exits non-zero (suggest exit code 2); (b) ON OBSERVATION of the auth flag, the script UNLINKS it (single-use) IMMEDIATELY — the unlink call appears BEFORE the sentinel-write attempt (codex finding #8 fix: iter-2-pre-codex try/finally wording was contradictory because finally runs AFTER the try block, not before). Single-use semantics: the grant is consumed at observation; sentinel-write success/failure does NOT affect the consumption — this matches user intent that bulk auth is per-explicit-action and not retry-able (OBJ-05 fix); (c) commands/commit.md Step 1 documents a BULK=true precondition check for the auth flag and aborts with the same message if absent (documentation-as-policy edit; runtime enforcement also lives in the script per (a)); (d) hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh (or hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh) denies ALL MODEL tool calls REGARDLESS OF agent_id (main agent AND subagent — codex finding #7 fix; main-agent exemption REMOVED) to create or write `/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag` OR `/tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json` via Bash (touch / echo > / cat > / printf > / tee > paths), Write, or NotebookEdit. The auth flag is only legitimately created by a human user typing directly in a terminal session (no model involvement, no hook trigger); (e) hooks/stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh (or another session-Stop hook) reaps any /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-<sid>.flag matching the closing session's ID; (f) the hook patch in (d) does NOT block the literal substring `--bulk` in agent text writes to non-/tmp/claude-bulk-* paths (specs, reports, tests may still contain the string); (g.script) ORCHESTRATOR-SIDE script-gate runtime test runs from main-agent / non-subagent context (the orchestrator dispatching this test has no `agent_id` and is therefore exempt from the M4(d) hook deny) and produces both outcomes deterministically: (g.script.1) auth-absent: script refuses + non-zero exit + correct stderr message; (g.script.2) auth-present: orchestrator `touch`es the flag (allowed because no agent_id in hook payload), then runs the script, then asserts: script writes sentinel + auth flag is unlinked + zero exit + sentinel file exists at the expected path; (g.script.3) auth-present-and-sentinel-write-FAILS: orchestrator `touch`es the flag then forces sentinel-write to fail (e.g., via env override of sentinel output dir to a read-only path, or stubbed file-write), asserts auth flag is STILL unlinked (try/finally verification per (b)); (g.hook) SYNTHETIC hook-payload unit test invokes the hook script(s) directly via stdin with mocked JSON payloads — asserts hook returns non-zero exit + deny message for subagent (agent_id present) writes to the protected globs, AND asserts hook returns zero exit when agent_id is absent (orchestrator exempt) OR when target path is outside the protected globs (no over-block). Per OBJ-02: the synthetic-payload approach eliminates the self-blocking contradiction where the test setup itself would be denied by the very hook it is testing.

check.required_literal_stderr_message: BULK mode requires explicit user authorization. The user (not an agent) must run: touch /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID.flag

required_script_behaviors._note: OBJ-05 + codex finding #8 fix: unlink call MUST appear BEFORE the sentinel-write call in main() — not inside a try/finally. Single-use observation-time semantics: the grant is consumed at observation regardless of whether the subsequent write succeeds. QA verifies BOTH (i) static source-read confirming the unlink-then-write ordering AND (ii) runtime test g.script.3 (sentinel-write forced to fail, assert flag is still unlinked because unlink happened FIRST). The unlink call MAY be wrapped in `try: os.unlink(); except OSError: pass` to be defensive against races, but the unlink call SITE is before the write call site, not after.

required_hook_deny_patterns.context: ALL model tool calls regardless of agent_id (codex finding #7 fix — main agent NO LONGER exempt; only the human user typing directly in a terminal can create the flag, and that path does not flow through Claude hooks)
ALL model tool calls regardless of agent_id (same rationale)

check._required_hook_deny_patterns_codex_note: Iter-2-post-codex (codex #7): the main-agent exemption was a real loophole because a model-controlled main agent could create the flag and proceed. The flag must originate from a human terminal session, not from any Claude model invocation. AC-04 g.hook case g_hook_5 was 'orchestrator exempt' in iter-2-pre-codex — that case is REMOVED in iter-2-post-codex; the hook must deny model-tool writes regardless of agent_id.

forbidden_hook_patterns.description: MUST NOT add a broad hook that blocks the literal text substring `--bulk` in agent text writes to non-/tmp/claude-bulk-* paths

runtime_test_g_script._context_note: Runs from ORCHESTRATOR / main-agent context ONLY (no agent_id in hook payload). The ONLY permitted execution mode is: the main-agent orchestrator directly executes the `touch /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}.flag` setup + the script invocation in its own Bash; QA subagent does NOT run g.script. There is NO `wrapper that strips agent_id` escape hatch — that was a Subagent Hook Discipline violation (CLAUDE.md rule §🛡️) and is removed in iter-2-post-codex. QA's role is: (1) READ the orchestrator's captured stdout/stderr/exit-code/post-state evidence from a known artifact path (e.g., `.claude/dev-registry/dev-${TASK_ID}/g-script-evidence.json`); (2) VALIDATE that evidence matches the expected per-case assertions; (3) INDEPENDENTLY run g.hook synthetic-payload tests (those are subagent-safe because they only invoke hook scripts with mocked JSON stdin — no real /tmp/claude-bulk-* writes occur).

case_g_script_1_auth_absent.expected_stderr_contains: BULK mode requires explicit user authorization

case_g_script_2_auth_present_write_succe.setup: Because the M4.3 hook now denies ALL model tool writes to /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-* regardless of agent_id (codex #7 fix), the main-agent ALSO cannot create the production-path flag via Bash. Setup MUST use the test-only path mechanism: scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py reads env var CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE; if set, the script checks/unlinks that path INSTEAD of /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-${sid}.flag. The hook only guards the production path; the test path is e.g. /tmp/claude-bulk-test-flag-${TASK_ID}-${RANDOM}.flag — outside the protected glob. Setup: `export CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE=/tmp/claude-bulk-test-flag-${TASK_ID}.flag; touch $CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE` (the touch goes through hook but the path is outside the protected glob, so allowed).
case_g_script_2_auth_present_write_succe.post_check_sentinel_exists_pattern: /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}-*.json (sentinel path is unaffected by override; the override only changes the AUTH FLAG path, not the sentinel output path — UNLESS dev decides to override sentinel path too, in which case the test asserts the overridden sentinel path; declare in dev-report)

case_g_script_3_auth_present_write_fails._purpose: OBJ-05 try/finally verification: assert auth flag is unlinked EVEN IF sentinel-write fails.
case_g_script_3_auth_present_write_fails.setup: export CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE=/tmp/claude-bulk-test-flag-${TASK_ID}.flag; touch $CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE; force sentinel-write to fail by either: (i) overriding the sentinel output directory env var to a non-writable path; (ii) chmod 0 on the sentinel target dir; (iii) monkey-patching json.dump in a wrapper test script
case_g_script_3_auth_present_write_fails._post_check_rationale: Per (b) the unlink MUST fire at observation time, before sentinel-write, in a try/finally finally-block. Even when sentinel-write raises, the auth flag MUST be gone afterward.
case_g_script_3_auth_present_write_fails.deviation_allowed_if_unforced_fail_impos: If the script does not currently support overriding the sentinel output dir AND the dev cannot easily simulate a write failure, dev MAY skip g.script.3 RUNTIME execution and instead document in the dev-report that try/finally unlink is verified by STATIC source-read of the script's main() implementation (the unlink call MUST appear inside a `finally:` block that wraps the sentinel-write `try:` block). QA accepts the static-read substitute IFF the dev-report includes the line numbers and verbatim try/finally code excerpt.

runtime_test_g_hook._context_note: Synthetic-payload unit test feeds mocked JSON payloads to the hook script's stdin and asserts exit code + stderr/stdout content. Isolates hook logic from script-gate logic and avoids the self-blocking contradiction.

payloads_to_send_to_hook_stdin._note: Iter-2-post-codex (codex finding #7): main-agent (agent_id absent) is NO LONGER exempt. Any model tool call writing the auth flag MUST be denied, regardless of agent_id. The auth flag is only legitimately created by a human user typing directly in a terminal (which does NOT flow through Claude hooks). Previously this case asserted exit zero — now asserts non-zero.
Hook MUST scope deny to /tmp/claude-bulk-* paths only; unrelated /tmp/ paths must be allowed (per M4.5 anti-overreach)

runtime_test_g_hook.harness_recipe: for each payload: `printf '%s' '<json>' | bash hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh` (or `python3 hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh` depending on channel and impl); capture exit code + stderr; assert per expected_hook_exit_*

criteria.evidence_required: static read of all five files + (g.script) runtime test stdout/stderr/exit-code for cases g.script.1, g.script.2, g.script.3 + (g.hook) synthetic-payload test stdout/stderr/exit-code for all 6 cases + verification of flag unlink (both success and failure paths) + verification of sentinel file existence

criteria.title: Spec-continue dispatch fires on non-bulk /commit fallback after blocked-bulk (M5 via R7)
criteria.given: AC-04 has landed (bulk auth gate active) AND commands/commit.md Step 7 is read AND a synthetic dirty git-tree with a real task-id is prepared AND TWO sequential test phases run: Phase A) /commit --bulk WITHOUT auth flag (must be blocked); Phase B) /commit <task-id> (non-bulk fallback, must succeed and fire Step 7)
criteria.when: the two phases are executed and Step 7 dispatch is observed
criteria.then: (a) Phase A: /commit --bulk without auth flag returns non-zero exit AND the script does NOT write a push-gate token (because no real commit was produced AND BULK=true would have skipped Step 7 anyway per commit.md:176-181); (b) Phase A produces no spec-continue dispatch (Step 7 SKIP confirmed); (c) Phase B: /commit <task-id> (no --bulk) on the still-dirty tree produces (c.1) a real commit (changelog-analyst returns committed status), (c.2) a push-gate token written at the expected path, (c.3) Step 7 spec-continue logic ACTUALLY EXECUTES — verified by either: (i) a test-mode env var like `COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1` that causes commit.md Step 7 to log a deterministic marker line such as `STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED: task-id=<id>` to stderr or to a trace log, OR (ii) observable side-effect (e.g., spec-continue Agent dispatch event recorded in `.claude/dev-registry/dev-<task-id>/step7-trace.json`); (d) the test deterministically distinguishes the two phases — Phase A: no Step 7, no spec-continue marker; Phase B: Step 7 fires, spec-continue marker emitted

check._purpose_note: OBJ-03 fix: iter-1 BA claimed R7 (spec-continue trigger) was implicit via AC-04. QA-iter1 confirmed by direct read of commit.md:176-181 that Step 7 SKIPS when BULK=true. This AC verifies the full chain: blocked-bulk → non-bulk fallback → Step 7 fires.

static_assertions_about_step_7._context: Step 7 SKIP condition must include BULK=true (existing behavior; asserting unchanged and observable)
Step 7 dispatch logic is present and not accidentally removed by M4 edits

preconditions: fresh test git repo with a dirty tree (uncommitted file)
runtime_test_phase_a_blocked_bulk.exec_simulation: Invoke the bulk-commit-sentinel write path (the script directly OR via a /commit --bulk simulation; orchestrator runs from non-subagent context per OBJ-02 split)
assertions: no file matching the push-gate token path was created
no spec-continue marker emitted to stderr / trace log

preconditions: same dirty tree from Phase A still uncommitted (in a SYNTHETIC test git repo — not the project working tree)
test-mode env var COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1 OR dev added an observable step7 trace mechanism per (c)(i)
TASK_ID set to the synthetic task-id
runtime_test_phase_b_non_bulk_fallback.exec_real_fallback_chain_required: Invoke /commit <task-id> (non-bulk path) END-TO-END on the synthetic dirty test repo. Iter-2-post-codex (codex finding #3): the stubbed Phase B option is REMOVED. The test MUST run the real commit.md Step 5/6/7 sequence — real changelog-analyst dispatch, real commit, real push-gate token write, real Step 7 dispatch evaluation. The synthetic repo isolates this from the live working tree.
runtime_test_phase_b_non_bulk_fallback.supplemental_unit_test_allowed: Dev MAY ADDITIONALLY include a unit test that exercises Step 7 logic in isolation with a stub push-gate token. This is SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY — it does NOT substitute for the real end-to-end Phase B test. The dev-report MUST clearly label the unit test as supplemental and the e2e test as primary.
assertions: Real commit SHA changed (new commit object in synthetic repo's git log)
changelog-analyst returned commit_status=committed (per its JSON contract)
Push-gate token written at the expected path
Step 7 SKIP did NOT fire (BULK=false, DRYRUN=false, TASK_ID present, push-gate token present)

deferral_clause._purpose: AC-05 may NOT be self-deferred by dev. Iter-2-post-codex tightens (codex finding #4): deferral requires a SEPARATE, CITABLE user message dated AFTER this ba-spec's `revised_at` timestamp that explicitly approves AC-05 deferral. The dev-report MUST cite the user message verbatim (or a path to the artifact storing it) AND QA MUST refuse to accept the deferral if that artifact is absent OR if the cited message does not literally approve AC-05 deferral.

schema.user_message_verbatim: string (the user's exact words approving deferral)
schema.user_message_artifact_path: string (path to the file containing the message, e.g. docs/dev/user-clarifications-20260524-205206.md)
schema.claude_session_id: string (the session in which the user provided consent)

deferral_evidence_required.qa_validation_rule: QA MUST grep the cited artifact path for the verbatim user_message string; QA MUST verify the timestamp is AFTER revision_metadata.revised_at; if either check fails, QA MUST mark AC-05 as fail (NOT deferred).

criteria._ac_deviation_allowed_note: Iter-2-post-codex (codex finding #4): ac_deviation_allowed downgraded from true to false. Deferral via deferral_clause is the ONLY exception, and it requires the dev-report to cite an external user-consent artifact per deferral_clause.deferral_evidence_required. Any other deviation is a contract violation.
criteria.evidence_required: static-read of commit.md Step 7 SKIP condition + Phase A runtime test (blocked bulk = no Step 7 marker) + Phase B runtime test (non-bulk fallback = Step 7 fires with observable marker) OR a fully-justified deferral record in the dev-report

_note_on_m5: AC-05 (new in iteration 2 per OBJ-03) supersedes the iteration-1 claim that M5 was implicit via AC-04. Direct read of commands/commit.md:176-181 confirmed Step 7 SKIPS when BULK=true, so the iter-1 implication chain was unverified. AC-05 now verifies the chain: blocked-bulk → non-bulk fallback → Step 7 actually fires.
_verification_harness_cleanup_contract_n: Per R10 (task 20260519-211515): any verification recipe spawning a background child process MUST install `trap '<cleanup>' EXIT INT TERM` BEFORE spawning. AC-02 test scripts, AC-04 runtime tests (g.script + g.hook), and AC-05 runtime tests (phase A + phase B) run SYNCHRONOUSLY without background spawns — no child processes to trap, contract satisfied trivially. If a future test refactor introduces a background daemon, apply the canonical pattern in agents/qa.md `### Verification harness cleanup contract (MANDATORY)`.
